content:
Post 1
ASX 200 closed at 8,547 points last week. The dominant theme is Fed rate path uncertainty colliding with resilient domestic employment. View is cautious — US 10-year yields at 4.42% keep pressure on rate-sensitive sectors and cap upside for the index.

Post 2
Materials and REITs are the two sectors to watch. Iron ore at USD 97 per tonne sets the tone for BHP and RIO into China data. For REITs, any yield curve shift hits valuations directly. Watch GMG — trading near 36x forward earnings, leaving little margin for a bond market surprise.

Post 3
The RBA minutes drop Tuesday 17 June. Markets are pricing roughly 62bps of cuts by year-end. A hawkish read — any signal the board debated holding — sends financials and rate-sensitives lower fast. A dovish tone lifts REITs and consumer discretionary, with the ASX 200 likely testing 8,600 points.
